target.name: Antora Energy
target.founder: Justin Briggs, Andrew Ponec, David Bierman

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $150M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Antora Energy is a Series B climate and energy company that has raised $150M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company (Lanzatech, ticker LNZA) in the climate and energy sector, including same-stage and adjacent-stage peers such as KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $150M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B to next-stage step-ups, implying a valuation of $285M. The peer-median method examines three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: KoBold Metals ($3.0B, Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B, Series C), and Amogy ($700M, Series C). The median valuation across these three peers is $1.0B, with a range of $700M to $3.0B.

Triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $242M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $642M. Key uncertainties include stage-appropriate discount relative to the Series C peer set—the peer median may overweight later-stage risk premiums—and the heterogeneity within climate tech, where technology type and capital intensity drive wide valuation dispersion. The $700M-to-$1.0B band represents the most defensible central estimate absent further stage-specific comparables or financial metrics.
